GlobalPolicy.module-level-focus: Fast deterministic checks close to the module with explicit async, auth, pagination, strict TypeScript, and FSD boundary assertions.
GlobalPolicy.wave-level-focus: Check only merged surfaces touched by the active wave, especially image upload, search interpretation, catalog browsing, admin session flow, and frontend FSD imports.
GlobalPolicy.phase-level-focus: Run broader integration and regression checks, including infra boot, async API behavior, frontend type-check/lint/build, and UI interaction smoke tests.
GlobalPolicy.frontend-static-first: Frontend module verification must pass type-check and FSD import linting before widget or page smoke checks are trusted.
GlobalPolicy.redaction: Never log secrets, credentials, JWTs, or raw high-risk payloads. Stable headers may expose parsed intent summaries but not sensitive internals.

VF-001.NAME: BrowseCatalog
VF-001.scenario: Visitor scrolls the public catalog past page 2.
VF-001.expected-outcome: API returns stable cursor-based pages with nearest_delivery_date and X-Total-Count header.

VF-002.NAME: UploadImage
VF-002.scenario: Admin uploads a product image and receives original plus thumbnail URLs.
VF-002.expected-outcome: Original and 300x300 JPEG thumbnail are stored in MinIO, keys are persisted, and presigned URLs are returned.

VF-003.NAME: AgentSearch
VF-003.scenario: External LLM agent searches for "беспроводные наушники до 5000".
VF-003.expected-outcome: Intent is parsed into name and max_price filters, results match ProductListItem shape, and X-Query-Interpreted exposes parsed intent.

VF-004.NAME: SeedDates
VF-004.scenario: Seed runs on a fresh database.
VF-004.expected-outcome: All delivery dates stay in the current week window and every product belongs to the demo merchant.

VF-005.NAME: FrontendCatalogUx
VF-005.scenario: Frontend user browses the catalog, opens a product modal, sorts offers, and replaces the grid with agent-search results.
VF-005.expected-outcome: FSD layers remain isolated, infinite scroll triggers correctly, ProductModal re-fetches on sort changes, and the agent-search handler can safely reset the visible product list.

VF-006.NAME: FutureMerchantIsolation
VF-006.scenario: Merchant admin attempts to access a product outside their merchant scope.
VF-006.expected-outcome: RBAC denies access before data leaves the repository/service chain.

VF-007.NAME: FrontendAdminSession
VF-007.scenario: Frontend admin logs in, loads the product table, and performs an authenticated image upload or CRUD mutation.
VF-007.expected-outcome: Login stores the Bearer token, protected routes redirect when unauthenticated, and all admin requests flow through the shared API client with auth headers attached.

VF-008.NAME: FrontendVoiceFallback
VF-008.scenario: Browser lacks Web Speech API support and the user still performs agent search through the text input.
VF-008.expected-outcome: Mic button stays hidden without errors, keyboard search still hits the agent endpoint, and the page shows interpreted intent when present.

module-checks.check-1: Manual review: all referenced module IDs and verification refs remain aligned.

scenarios.scenario-1: Every planned module has a matching graph node or future stub.

V-M-GOVERNANCE.wave-follow-up: Run grace-refresh or equivalent artifact sync when architecture changes.
V-M-GOVERNANCE.phase-follow-up: Run grace-reviewer with 0 critical issues before phase completion.

module-checks.check-1: Static review: all required env vars for DB, S3, auth, CORS, and feature flags are present.

scenarios.scenario-1: Compose and backend config can start from the env template without missing variables.

V-M-INFRA-ENV.wave-follow-up: Validate config parsing in backend tests.
V-M-INFRA-ENV.phase-follow-up: Keep env template aligned with deployment docs.

scenarios.scenario-1: Backend starts after postgres healthcheck and data volumes are bind-mounted outside containers.

V-M-INFRA-COMPOSE.wave-follow-up: Bring stack up before backend/frontend phase verification.
V-M-INFRA-COMPOSE.phase-follow-up: Confirm hot reload for both services.

scenarios.scenario-1: Valid env produces settings object with derived service URLs.
scenarios.scenario-2: Missing secret or malformed URL fails fast.

V-M-CONFIG.phase-follow-up: Confirm all runtime secrets still come from env.

scenarios.scenario-1: FastAPI app boots with CORS, all routers mounted, /health and /llms.txt accessible.
scenarios.scenario-2: /llms.txt returns plain text manifest per llmstxt.org spec.

V-M-APP.wave-follow-up: Run app boot test after any router or middleware changes.
V-M-APP.phase-follow-up: Confirm /llms.txt stays at app root and remains in sync with agent capabilities.

scenarios.scenario-1: AsyncSession dependency yields usable session.
scenarios.scenario-2: Sync driver or blocking call is rejected by review.

V-M-DB.wave-follow-up: Run async API tests against a live postgres container.
V-M-DB.phase-follow-up: Audit all handlers for sync DB calls.

scenarios.scenario-1: Default admin credentials produce a platform_admin JWT.
scenarios.scenario-2: Invalid credentials or invalid tokens do not produce a privileged payload.

V-M-AUTH.wave-follow-up: Exercise admin auth routes through the API layer after auth changes.
V-M-AUTH.phase-follow-up: Extend tests when merchant_admin and merchant_id claims are added.

scenarios.scenario-1: Phase 1 schema includes merchants, agent_requests, product merchant_id, status, and search_vector.

V-M-MIGRATIONS.wave-follow-up: Inspect generated SQL or resulting columns on a fresh DB.
V-M-MIGRATIONS.phase-follow-up: Confirm Phase 2 prep columns match the knowledge graph.

scenarios.scenario-1: Product model exposes merchant_id, status, and search_vector for Phase 2 prep.
scenarios.scenario-2: Seller, offer, and product attribute tables expose the expected catalog schema.

V-M-MODELS-CATALOG.wave-follow-up: Keep model columns aligned with the Phase 1 migration file.
V-M-MODELS-CATALOG.phase-follow-up: Extend assertions when merchant-scoped catalog logic lands.

scenarios.scenario-1: Merchant model has id, name, slug, is_active, created_at, updated_at.
scenarios.scenario-2: AgentRequest model has endpoint, query, interpreted_intent, result_count, response_ms, created_at.

V-M-MODELS-PLATFORM.phase-follow-up: Confirm Phase 2 prep columns are present.

scenarios.scenario-1: Offers sorted by price or delivery_date with deterministic secondary sort.
scenarios.scenario-2: Seller name is joined and returned with offer data.

V-M-REPO-OFFER.wave-follow-up: Verify API responses use stable sorting.

scenarios.scenario-1: CRUD operations for products, sellers, offers work correctly.
scenarios.scenario-2: save_product_image_keys persists object keys without DB errors.

V-M-REPO-ADMIN.wave-follow-up: Run admin API integration tests against repo layer.
V-M-REPO-ADMIN.phase-follow-up: Extend coverage when merchant scoping lands.

scenarios.scenario-1: Cursor pagination returns stable next_cursor and nearest_delivery_date.
scenarios.scenario-2: Repository avoids N+1 query growth past page 2.

V-M-REPO-PRODUCT.wave-follow-up: Exercise public API and agent search endpoints against seeded data.
V-M-REPO-PRODUCT.phase-follow-up: Add merchant scope assertions once Phase 2 starts.

scenarios.scenario-1: Original and thumbnail uploads succeed and return presigned URLs.
scenarios.scenario-2: Unsupported or broken images fail cleanly without partial DB writes.

V-M-S3.wave-follow-up: Run end-to-end upload through admin API against MinIO.
V-M-S3.phase-follow-up: Review image size and format constraints.

scenarios.scenario-1: Public list and detail responses are shaped correctly.
scenarios.scenario-2: Missing product id returns stable not-found handling.

V-M-SVC-PRODUCT.wave-follow-up: Verify API responses and frontend mappings stay aligned.
V-M-SVC-PRODUCT.phase-follow-up: Confirm agent search reuses the same item projection.

scenarios.scenario-1: Offer sorting remains available as a dedicated async service entrypoint.
scenarios.scenario-2: Sorting signals do not disappear from the service contract when repository behavior changes.

V-M-SVC-OFFER.wave-follow-up: Exercise price and delivery-date ordering through the public detail API.
V-M-SVC-OFFER.phase-follow-up: Extend coverage when merchant-specific ranking rules are introduced.

scenarios.scenario-1: Image upload persists both object keys after successful storage writes.
scenarios.scenario-2: DB update does not report success if storage upload fails.

V-M-SVC-ADMIN.wave-follow-up: Run admin API integration test with real MinIO.
V-M-SVC-ADMIN.phase-follow-up: Extend coverage when merchant scoping lands.

scenarios.scenario-1: Price/name intent parsing yields expected structured filters.
scenarios.scenario-2: Unparseable free text falls back safely without crashing.

V-M-SVC-AGENT.wave-follow-up: Check X-Query-Interpreted header and agent_requests writes via API tests.
V-M-SVC-AGENT.phase-follow-up: Deepen trace coverage when search sophistication increases.

scenarios.scenario-1: List endpoint returns {items, next_cursor} and X-Total-Count header.
scenarios.scenario-2: Detail endpoint handles missing products with stable error semantics.

V-M-API-PUBLIC.wave-follow-up: Run against seeded DB and exercise page 2 browse flow.
V-M-API-PUBLIC.phase-follow-up: Sample logs to prove async-only request paths.

scenarios.scenario-1: Correct credentials return Bearer token with configured expiry.
scenarios.scenario-2: Wrong credentials return 401.

V-M-API-ADMIN-AUTH.wave-follow-up: Verify frontend auth feature stores and reuses JWT.
V-M-API-ADMIN-AUTH.phase-follow-up: Check future merchant claims do not break platform admin login.

scenarios.scenario-1: Protected routes require Bearer auth and image upload returns presigned URLs.
scenarios.scenario-2: Unauthorized request is rejected without side effects.

V-M-API-ADMIN-CATALOG.wave-follow-up: Exercise end-to-end upload against live MinIO.
V-M-API-ADMIN-CATALOG.phase-follow-up: Add merchant authorization checks in Phase 2.

scenarios.scenario-1: llms.txt, /v1/agent/context, and /v1/agent/search all return the required payload shapes.
scenarios.scenario-2: Malformed agent search input returns stable validation errors.

V-M-API-AGENT.phase-follow-up: Review audit log density and redaction.

scenarios.scenario-1: Seed creates demo merchant and assigns every product to it.
scenarios.scenario-2: Generated delivery_date values never exceed today + 6 days.

V-M-SEED.wave-follow-up: Re-run after schema changes that affect seed shape.
V-M-SEED.phase-follow-up: Keep counts and distributions aligned with requirements.

scenarios.scenario-1: Vite alias, strict TypeScript, lint rules, env defaults, and Docker startup are configured coherently.
scenarios.scenario-2: Lint or type-check fails fast when the alias map or FSD import rules are broken.

V-M-FE-TOOLING.wave-follow-up: Static tooling must pass before any later frontend wave is trusted.
V-M-FE-TOOLING.phase-follow-up: Keep dependency versions aligned with technology.xml and build constraints.

scenarios.scenario-1: The shared client applies base URL, timeout, and auth header injection correctly.
scenarios.scenario-2: 401 responses clear the stored token and redirect to /admin/login; non-401 responses do not redirect.
scenarios.scenario-3: Agent search returns typed product results and captures X-Query-Interpreted from headers.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: No frontend module outside src/shared/api may instantiate axios or use raw fetch.

V-M-FE-SHARED-API.wave-follow-up: Ensure later entity and page modules use only this shared API surface.
V-M-FE-SHARED-API.phase-follow-up: Recheck redirect semantics against the final router guards.

scenarios.scenario-1: Button, Badge, and Spinner render with typed props and no embedded business logic.

V-M-FE-SHARED-UI.wave-follow-up: Verify widget consumers do not reimplement the same primitives.
V-M-FE-SHARED-UI.phase-follow-up: Review accessibility semantics of the shared controls.

scenarios.scenario-1: formatPrice and formatDate return deterministic ru-RU-friendly strings for representative inputs.

V-M-FE-SHARED-LIB.wave-follow-up: Verify widgets use only the shared formatter exports.
V-M-FE-SHARED-LIB.phase-follow-up: Keep locale assumptions explicit if other locales are added later.

scenarios.scenario-1: Product list, detail, and admin DTOs are typed and match backend contract shapes.
scenarios.scenario-2: fetchProducts parses X-Total-Count and cursor values correctly.
scenarios.scenario-3: adminUploadImage sends multipart/form-data and returns typed image URLs.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Product entity files must not import offer or seller slices directly.

V-M-FE-ENTITIES-PRODUCT.wave-follow-up: Re-run against live seeded backend payloads.
V-M-FE-ENTITIES-PRODUCT.phase-follow-up: Extend DTO coverage when merchant-specific fields are exposed.

scenarios.scenario-1: Offer and OfferSort exports remain stable for widget and feature consumers.

V-M-FE-ENTITIES-OFFER.wave-follow-up: Confirm modal sorting continues to use only these public exports.
V-M-FE-ENTITIES-OFFER.phase-follow-up: Broaden offer shape checks if backend pricing contracts expand.

scenarios.scenario-1: Seller exports remain pure typed contracts with no shared API coupling.

V-M-FE-ENTITIES-SELLER.wave-follow-up: Verify offers and admin tables consume only the public seller exports.
V-M-FE-ENTITIES-SELLER.phase-follow-up: Extend seller typing if ownership or rating semantics change.

scenarios.scenario-1: The first page loads on mount and subsequent pages load only when the sentinel intersects.
scenarios.scenario-2: Concurrent load attempts are suppressed while a request is already in flight.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: BLOCK_LOAD_PAGE must not re-enter while isLoading is true.

V-M-FE-FEATURE-SCROLL.wave-follow-up: Verify CatalogPage uses reset() only after agent search results are returned.
V-M-FE-FEATURE-SCROLL.phase-follow-up: Run live page smoke checks against the catalog endpoint.

scenarios.scenario-1: Sort state toggles deterministically between price and delivery_date and SortButtons emit the public values.

V-M-FE-FEATURE-SORT.wave-follow-up: Confirm ProductModal re-fetches when this feature emits a new value.
V-M-FE-FEATURE-SORT.phase-follow-up: Keep sort key surface aligned with backend offers_sort support.

scenarios.scenario-1: Successful login stores the token and redirects to /admin.
scenarios.scenario-2: Failed login leaves the token empty and exposes the stable error message.

V-M-FE-FEATURE-AUTH.wave-follow-up: Verify page-level admin guards and shared client interceptors are consistent with this feature.
V-M-FE-FEATURE-AUTH.phase-follow-up: Audit token handling and logout behavior against the final router flow.

scenarios.scenario-1: VoiceSearchButton stays hidden when SpeechRecognition is unavailable.
scenarios.scenario-2: Text-based agent search still works when voice support is absent.
scenarios.scenario-3: Recognition errors stop listening without surfacing user-facing exceptions.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: BLOCK_AGENT_SEARCH may execute without BLOCK_RECOGNITION_SETUP when Enter-triggered text search is used.

V-M-FE-FEATURE-VOICE.wave-follow-up: Connect voice feature to CatalogPage and live agent endpoint doubles.
V-M-FE-FEATURE-VOICE.phase-follow-up: Run one smoke check in a browser with and without speech support.

scenarios.scenario-1: ProductCard renders pricing, stock, and nearest delivery and emits the selected id on click.

V-M-FE-WIDGET-CARD.wave-follow-up: Verify CatalogPage uses only the public widget export.
V-M-FE-WIDGET-CARD.phase-follow-up: Review responsive behavior inside the catalog grid.

scenarios.scenario-1: ProductModal fetches details when productId is set and re-fetches on sort changes.
scenarios.scenario-2: No detail request is sent when productId is null.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: BLOCK_FETCH_ON_ID_CHANGE must short-circuit when productId is null.

V-M-FE-WIDGET-MODAL.wave-follow-up: Exercise modal sorting against live offers_sort backend behavior.
V-M-FE-WIDGET-MODAL.phase-follow-up: Review focus trap and close behavior when integrated into the page.

scenarios.scenario-1: ProductsTable emits edit, delete, and uploadImage actions with the correct product id.

V-M-FE-WIDGET-TABLE.wave-follow-up: Verify AdminPage wires these emits to the proper entity API calls.
V-M-FE-WIDGET-TABLE.phase-follow-up: Review table interaction states with real admin data.

module-checks.check-2: npm exec vitest run src/pages/catalog src/pages/product

scenarios.scenario-1: CatalogPage wires infinite scroll, modal selection, and agent search reset behavior correctly.
scenarios.scenario-2: Agent search on Enter replaces the grid with returned ProductListItem values and shows interpreted intent when present.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: CatalogPage reset() must receive only ProductListItem arrays returned from public feature/entity APIs.

V-M-FE-PAGE-CATALOG.wave-follow-up: Run live smoke checks against catalog and agent backend endpoints.
V-M-FE-PAGE-CATALOG.phase-follow-up: Keep the thin ProductPage fallback aligned with modal data needs if it stays in the route tree.

scenarios.scenario-1: LoginPage submits credentials through useAdminAuth and shows stable failures.
scenarios.scenario-2: AdminPage redirects unauthenticated users and loads products when a token is present.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: AdminPage must not call admin endpoints before auth state or route guard validation.

V-M-FE-PAGE-ADMIN.wave-follow-up: Exercise admin flows against auth and catalog backend doubles, then live endpoints.
V-M-FE-PAGE-ADMIN.phase-follow-up: Review CRUD and image upload UI states with the final admin API surface.

scenarios.scenario-1: Vue bootstrap registers router and Pinia without cross-layer leaks.
scenarios.scenario-2: Route guards redirect unauthenticated admin access to /admin/login before page loading occurs.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: BLOCK_ROUTE_GUARDS must redirect before AdminPage data loading when the token is missing.

V-M-FE-APP.wave-follow-up: Smoke-test browser startup against the Compose backend proxy on port 5173.
V-M-FE-APP.phase-follow-up: Confirm build output and base-route assumptions before marking Phase 1b complete.

module-checks.check-1: Manual review: RBAC remains claim-based and merchant-scoped before implementation begins.

scenarios.scenario-1: Merchant claims allow only same-merchant access.
scenarios.scenario-2: Missing merchant_id denies merchant-admin requests.

V-M-RBAC.wave-follow-up: Add executable tests when app/core/rbac.py exists.
V-M-RBAC.phase-follow-up: Expand roles for support and operations actors once runtime code lands.

module-checks.check-1: Manual review: merchant portal remains scoped through merchant_id and RBAC.

scenarios.scenario-1: Merchant admin sees only own products.

V-M-MERCHANT.wave-follow-up: Add executable tests when app/services/merchant_portal_service.py exists.
V-M-MERCHANT.phase-follow-up: Pen-test cross-merchant access paths once runtime code lands.

module-checks.check-1: Manual review: future search module stays compatible with current product schema and interpreted intent flow.

scenarios.scenario-1: Advanced search extends the current interpreted filters without breaking Phase 1 contracts.

V-M-SEARCH.wave-follow-up: Add executable tests when app/services/search_service.py exists.
V-M-SEARCH.phase-follow-up: Promote to runtime verification before Phase 2 search work is marked complete.

module-checks.check-1: Manual review: recommendation integration remains optional and feature-flagged.

scenarios.scenario-1: Recommendation features stay isolated behind config flags and do not become a Phase 1 runtime dependency.

V-M-RECOMMENDATIONS.wave-follow-up: Add executable tests when app/services/recommendations_service.py exists.
V-M-RECOMMENDATIONS.phase-follow-up: Promote to runtime verification before recommendation work is marked complete.

module-checks.check-1: Manual review: future order flow preserves Phase 1 catalog and merchant contracts.

scenarios.scenario-1: Order lifecycle work layers on top of the prepared schema instead of forcing a structural rewrite.

V-M-ORDERS.wave-follow-up: Add executable tests when app/services/orders_service.py exists.
V-M-ORDERS.phase-follow-up: Promote to runtime verification before order work is marked complete.

module-checks.check-1: Manual review: richer agent audit work remains layered on the existing agent_requests table.

scenarios.scenario-1: Enhanced agent audit features extend the prepared schema without breaking Phase 1 agent search logging.

V-M-AGENT-AUDIT.wave-follow-up: Add executable tests when app/services/agent_audit_service.py exists.
V-M-AGENT-AUDIT.phase-follow-up: Promote to runtime verification before Phase 2 audit work is marked complete.

Gate-Phase-0.goal: Bootstrap artifacts exist and the project can move into implementation with a stable plan.

required-evidence.evidence-1: AGENTS.md, GRACE XML docs, docker-compose.yml, .env.example, and docs/ai files are present.

Gate-Phase-1.goal: Backend prototype satisfies async runtime, schema foresight, and public/admin/agent API correctness.

required-evidence.evidence-1: All critical backend checklist items pass, including async-only handlers and required schema columns.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Seed and agent search flows produce the expected evidence markers.

Gate-Phase-1b.goal: Frontend FSD layers, infinite scroll, modal sorting, agent bar, and admin auth work against the backend.

required-evidence.evidence-2: No raw axios or fetch calls exist outside src/shared/api/client.ts and its public wrappers.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Frontend checklist items pass against live backend data or stable API doubles where the backend surface is not yet merged.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Catalog browse, ProductModal sort re-fetch, voice fallback, agent Enter search, and admin login redirect each have deterministic module-level evidence plus one page-level smoke path.

Gate-Phase-2.goal: Merchant isolation, extended agent audit, search, and optional recommendations expand without breaking Phase 1 contracts.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Cross-merchant access is denied and future modules use the prepared schema instead of requiring structural rework.
